evals.prompt: I just ran npm install -g @sporhq/spor and spor install claude in my terminal, but nothing seems to happen when I work in my repo. How do I actually get this set up?
evals.expected_output: Triggers onboard. Runs `spor status` first, detects local vs remote, and walks the right setup branch (init -> person create -> agent create/use -> enable), checking the Node-runtime / plugin-stale lines, then hands off to /spor:backfill.

evals.prompt: My teammate sent me a Spor server URL and said to join the team graph. Can you connect me?
evals.expected_output: Triggers onboard, takes the REMOTE branch: spor auth login --server <url> (or spor join), pick the org, then VERIFY the token binds to a person via spor whoami and surface '⚠ token maps to no person' if unbound, then spor enable.

evals.prompt: Set up spor for me from scratch on this laptop — I don't have a server, I just want it tracking my own work locally.
evals.expected_output: Triggers onboard, takes the LOCAL branch: spor init (check the commits: identity line), spor person create, spor agent create + spor agent use, spor enable, states the local-only data caveat correctly, then hands off to /spor:backfill.

evals.prompt: Can you backfill the git history of this repo into the spor graph and group my repos into projects?
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger onboard — this is /spor:backfill's job (populate + group), and an identity already exists. onboard is the first-time identity door, not the populate door.

evals.prompt: What's the difference between a decision node and an artifact node in spor, and when should I use each?
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger onboard — this is a conceptual question about the ontology, handled by /spor:spor, not a setup task.
